zotero.abstractNote: Learning a prior over articulated poses is a fundamental problem in computer vision, with applications ranging from motion capture to pose estimation. Existing methods often rely on simple parametric models or data-driven approaches that struggle to generalize to unseen poses or complex articulations. In this paper, we introduce Neural Riemannian Distance Fields (NRDF), a novel representation for learning articulated pose priors. NRDF represents the pose distribution as a distance field defined on a Riemannian manifold, where the distance to the manifold of valid poses is learned. By leveraging the Riemannian structure, NRDF can effectively capture the geometry of the pose space and provide a robust prior for various downstream tasks. We demonstrate the effectiveness of NRDF on several benchmarks, showing that it outperforms existing methods in terms of both accuracy and generalization.
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the IEEE/CVF Conference on Computer Vision and Pattern Recognition (CVPR)
